info.title: CoLD API — Choice of Law Dataverse
info.description:
# Choice of Law Dataverse (CoLD) API

Open-access REST API for the **[Choice of Law Dataverse](https://cold.global)**, a curated knowledge base on choice of law in international contracts. CoLD is an SNF-funded project at the University of Lucerne, licensed under [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Winner of the Swiss National ORD Prize 2025.

## Available datasets

| Table name | Description |
|---|---|
| **Answers** | Country-level responses to the CoLD standardised questionnaire on choice-of-law rules |
| **HCCH Answers** | Answers mapped to the HCCH Principles on Choice of Law |
| **Questions** | The standardised questionnaire items that Answers respond to |
| **Court Decisions** | Case law with metadata, themes, PIL provisions, and jurisdiction links |
| **Domestic Instruments** | National statutes, codes, and PIL regulations |
| **Domestic Legal Provisions** | Individual articles/provisions within domestic instruments |
| **Regional Instruments** | Supranational instruments (e.g. EU Rome I Regulation) |
| **Regional Legal Provisions** | Individual articles/provisions within regional instruments |
| **International Instruments** | Treaties, conventions, and model laws (e.g. HCCH Principles) |
| **International Legal Provisions** | Individual articles/provisions within international instruments |
| **Literature** | Academic and practitioner publications on choice of law |
| **Arbitral Awards** | Published arbitral awards with choice-of-law analysis |
| **Arbitral Rules** | Institutional arbitration rules (e.g. ICC, LCIA) |
| **Arbitral Provisions** | Individual articles within arbitral rules |
| **Arbitral Institutions** | Arbitration institutions (e.g. ICC, SIAC) |
| **Jurisdictions** | Countries and territories with metadata (region, legal family) |
| **Specialists** | Choice-of-law experts by jurisdiction |

Bulk CSV/XLSX exports are also available at [cold.global/data-sets](https://cold.global/data-sets).

## Quick start for data scientists

1. **Search** — `GET /api/v1/search/?search_string=party+autonomy` returns paginated results across all datasets.
2. **Bulk export** — `GET /api/v1/search/full_table?table=Court+Decisions` returns every record in a table (see table names above).
3. **Record detail** — `GET /api/v1/search/details?table=Court+Decisions&id=CD-CHE-42` returns a single record with its related entities.
4. **Entity lists** — `GET /api/v1/entities/court-decisions?jurisdiction=CHE` returns paginated, filterable entity catalogues.
5. **Statistics** — `GET /api/v1/statistics/counts` returns record counts per entity type, optionally scoped to a jurisdiction.

## Glossary

Key private international law terms used throughout the API (party autonomy, dépeçage, PIL codification, mandatory rules, etc.) are defined in the [CoLD Glossary](https://cold.global/learn/glossary).

## Authentication

**Read-only data endpoints are publicly accessible — no API key or token required.**

Write endpoints (submitting suggestions, feedback, case analysis) require an Auth0 JWT in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Moderation endpoints additionally require editor or admin roles.

get.summary: Full-text search across all CoLD datasets
get.description:
Searches across all CoLD datasets: Answers, HCCH Answers, Court Decisions, Domestic/Regional/International Instruments and their Legal Provisions, Literature, Arbitral Awards, Arbitral Rules, Arbitral Provisions, Arbitral Institutions, Jurisdictions, and Questions.

Results are ranked by relevance and returned in a paginated envelope. Use the repeatable query parameters `tables`, `jurisdictions`, and `themes` to narrow results. Pass each filter value as a separate parameter, e.g. `?tables=Answers&tables=Court+Decisions&jurisdictions=Switzerland`.

Set `sort_by_date=true` to order results chronologically (newest first) instead of by relevance.

parameters.name: sort_by_date

schema.description: Sort results by date descending if True.
schema.title: Sort By Date

parameters.description: Sort results by date descending if True.

200.description: Search results including pagination and total matches.

get.summary: Fetch a single record by CoLD ID with related entities
get.description: Returns the full detail for one record, identified by its source table and CoLD ID (e.g. `table=Court+Decisions&id=CD-CHE-42`). The response includes the record's own fields plus all first-hop relations (linked jurisdictions, themes, instruments, literature, etc.) in a standardised shape. Ideal for building detail pages or enriching search results.

schema.title: Response Handle Entity Detail Api V1 Search Details Get

get.summary: Bulk export: return a full table or filtered subset
get.description:
Returns every record from the specified table, or a filtered subset. This is the primary endpoint for **bulk data export**.

**Available tables:** Answers, HCCH Answers, Questions, Court Decisions, Domestic Instruments, Domestic Legal Provisions, Regional Instruments, Regional Legal Provisions, International Instruments, International Legal Provisions, Literature, Arbitral Awards, Arbitral Rules, Arbitral Provisions, Arbitral Institutions, Jurisdictions, Specialists.

Filters use the repeatable `filter` query parameter in `column:value` format (or `column:val1,val2` for OR). Values matching `true`/`false` are coerced to booleans; pure-digit strings to integers; everything else stays a string.

Example: `?table=Court+Decisions&filter=caseRank:10&filter=jurisdiction:Switzerland`.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Omit for no limit.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Omit for no limit.

parameters.name: order_by

schema.description: Column name to sort by (camelCase or snake_case). Unknown columns are ignored.
schema.title: Order By

parameters.description: Column name to sort by (camelCase or snake_case). Unknown columns are ignored.

parameters.name: order_dir

schema.description: Sort direction when order_by is provided.
schema.title: Order Dir

parameters.description: Sort direction when order_by is provided.

200.description: Array of transformed records from the requested table.

schema.title: Response Return Full Table Api V1 Search Full Table Get

500.description: Server error while querying the table.

get.description: Returns all choice-of-law specialists associated with a jurisdiction, identified by its ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 code (e.g. `CHE` for Switzerland, `GBR` for the United Kingdom).

200.description: Array of specialists for the given jurisdiction.

schema.title: Response Get Specialists By Jurisdiction Api V1 Search Specialists  Jurisdiction Alpha Code  Get

get.summary: Classify a free-text user query into a predefined category
get.description: Maps a free-text question to the most relevant predefined CoLD thematic category (e.g. 'Party autonomy (concept)', 'Mandatory rules', 'Renvoi'). Uses an LLM under the hood. The returned string can be fed back into the search endpoint's `themes` filter to retrieve matching records.

parameters.name: query

schema.description: Free-text query to classify, e.g. 'How do courts treat party autonomy?'
schema.title: Query

parameters.description: Free-text query to classify, e.g. 'How do courts treat party autonomy?'

200.description: The matched CoLD category as a plain string.

schema.title: Response Classify Query Get Api V1 Ai Classify Query Get

502.description: The upstream AI service returned an error or timed out.

post.summary: Upload a court decision PDF for initial processing
post.description:
Upload a PDF court decision (base64-encoded or as an Azure blob URL). The system will:

1. **Upload to storage** — decode and persist the PDF in Azure Blob Storage
2. **Validate text extraction** — confirm the PDF parses to machine-readable text
3. **Save draft** — persist the draft in the database for subsequent analysis

Returns a **Server-Sent Events (SSE)** stream with progress updates and periodic heartbeats. The final event contains the `draft_id`. Jurisdiction detection runs during `/analyze` in parallel with the choice-of-law section extraction. Maximum PDF size: 50 MB. Requires authentication.

200.description: SSE stream of processing steps. Final event includes `draft_id`.

post.description:
Trigger the full analysis workflow on an uploaded draft. The system runs multiple LLM-powered extraction steps; jurisdiction detection and choice-of-law section extraction run concurrently as stage 1, then the dependent steps fan out:

- **Jurisdiction detection** (parallel with col_section, unless `jurisdiction` is provided in the body as a user override from the Review & Submit screen)
- **Choice-of-law section** extraction
- **Theme** classification
- **Case citation** extraction
- **Relevant facts** extraction
- **PIL provisions** extraction
- **Choice-of-law issue** extraction
- **Court's position** extraction
- **Obiter dicta** and **dissenting opinions** (common-law and Indian decisions only)
- **Abstract** generation

Returns a **Server-Sent Events (SSE)** stream with each step's result as it completes. The draft is updated in the database after each step for crash recovery. Set `resume=true` to skip already-completed steps (e.g. after a network interruption). Requires authentication.

200.description: SSE stream of analysis steps. Final event has `analysis_complete` status.

post.summary: Submit case analysis for moderation review
post.description: Submit user-reviewed and edited case analysis data for moderator approval. The endpoint validates ownership, stores the edited data alongside the original AI-generated output (preserved for audit), and sets the status to `pending`. Moderators can then approve or reject the submission. Requires authentication.

400.description: Draft already submitted or in a non-submittable state.

401.description: Unable to identify the authenticated user.

403.description: The draft belongs to a different user.

get.summary: List the authenticated user's case analyses
get.description: Returns a summary of every case analysis draft created by the authenticated user, regardless of status (draft, analyzing, completed, pending, approved, rejected). Requires authentication.

schema.title: Response List My Analyses Api V1 Case Analyzer My Analyses Get

get.description: Fetch the full draft data (jurisdiction info, analyzer step results, PDF URL) to restore the client-side form after a page reload or network interruption. Only available for drafts that have not yet been submitted for moderation (i.e. not in `pending`, `approved`, or `rejected` status). Requires authentication.

400.description: Draft already submitted and cannot be recovered for editing.

403.description: The draft belongs to a different user.

get.description: Returns every public-facing URL for the CoLD frontend. Intended for generating XML sitemaps for search engine indexing.

200.description: Array of URL entries with loc and optional lastmod/priority.

schema.title: Response Get All Frontend Urls Api V1 Sitemap Urls Get

get.description: Returns ISO Alpha-3 codes and a has_data flag (1/0) depending on whether non-'No data' answers exist.

200.description: Array of jurisdictions and availability flag.

schema.title: Response Get Jurisdictions Api V1 Landing Page Jurisdictions Get

get.description: Returns every jurisdiction with its metadata (name, Alpha-3 code, legal family) and an `answer_coverage` percentage indicating how many of the standardised questionnaire questions have substantive answers (i.e. not 'No data'). Useful for assessing data completeness across jurisdictions.

200.description: Array of jurisdictions with all fields plus answer_coverage.

schema.title: Response Get Jurisdictions With Answer Coverage Api V1 Statistics Jurisdictions With Answer Percentage Get

get.summary: Record counts grouped by jurisdiction for a table
get.description: Returns the number of records per jurisdiction for the given table. Supported tables: **Court Decisions**, **Domestic Instruments**, **Literature**. Useful for visualising geographic distribution of data (e.g. choropleth maps).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Optional limit on number of results to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Optional limit on number of results to return

200.description: Array of jurisdictions with their counts.

schema.title: Response Count By Jurisdiction Api V1 Statistics Count By Jurisdiction Get

get.description: Returns the total number of records for each entity type in the dataverse (Court Decisions, Domestic Instruments, Literature, etc.). Pass an optional `jurisdiction` Alpha-3 code to scope counts to a single country. Useful for dashboard summaries and data completeness overviews.

parameters.name: jurisdiction

schema.description: Optional Alpha-3 code; only counts entities tagged to it.
schema.title: Jurisdiction

parameters.description: Optional Alpha-3 code; only counts entities tagged to it.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of entities for the given slug. Each item uses the slim relation shape (the same fields embedded in detail responses).

**Available slugs:** `court-decisions`, `domestic-instruments`, `regional-instruments`, `international-instruments`, `literature`, `arbitral-awards`, `arbitral-rules`, `arbitral-institutions`, `jurisdictions`, `specialists`, `questions`.

Filter by `jurisdiction` (Alpha-3 code) or `theme` (substring match). Court Decisions additionally accept a `case_rank` filter. Results are sorted by title by default; override with `order_by` and `order_dir`.

parameters.name: case_rank

schema.description: Court Decisions only: filter by case rank.
schema.title: Case Rank

parameters.description: Court Decisions only: filter by case rank.

parameters.name: order_by

schema.description: Override default order column (snake or camelCase).
schema.title: Order By

parameters.description: Override default order column (snake or camelCase).

post.description: Submit a free-form data contribution for moderator review. The payload is stored as-is in a separate suggestions table. Use the typed endpoints below (court-decisions, domestic-instruments, etc.) for structured submissions. Requires authentication.

post.summary: Submit a new Court Decision suggestion
Submit a new Domestic Instrument suggestion
Submit a new Regional Instrument suggestion
Submit a new International Instrument suggestion

get.description: Returns the number of pending suggestions in each moderation category (court decisions, instruments, literature, case analyzer, feedback). Requires editor or admin role.

schema.title: Response Moderation Summary Api V1 Suggestions Moderation Summary Get

get.summary: List pending suggestions in a category
get.description:
Returns all pending suggestions for the specified category slug. For `case-analyzer`, set `show_all=true` to include non-pending items. Requires editor or admin role.

**Category slugs:** `case-analyzer`, `court-decisions`, `domestic-instruments`, `regional-instruments`, `international-instruments`, `literature`, `feedback`.

schema.title: Response List Pending Suggestions Api V1 Suggestions Pending  Category  Get

get.description: Authenticated users can retrieve details for their own submissions. Editors and admins can access any suggestion for moderation.

delete.description: Permanently remove a suggestion. Only supported for the `case-analyzer` category. Requires editor or admin role.

post.description: Mark a suggestion as approved and insert its data into the main CoLD database. For case-analyzer suggestions, this runs the full approval pipeline. For other categories, the payload is written directly to the target table. Requires editor or admin role.
Mark a suggestion as rejected. The record is preserved for audit but not inserted into CoLD. Requires editor or admin role.

Report an error, suggest a correction, or leave a comment on an existing CoLD record. Authentication is optional — anonymous submissions are accepted but authenticated users can track their submissions.

get.description: Returns all feedback items awaiting moderation. Requires editor or admin role.

schema.title: Response List Pending Api V1 Feedback Pending Get

get.description: Retrieve full details for a single feedback item. Requires editor or admin role.

patch.description: Change the moderation status of a feedback item (e.g. pending → resolved). Requires editor or admin role.

get.description: Returns a simple message confirming the API is running.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Core data-access endpoints. Full-text search across all datasets with faceted filtering (by table, jurisdiction, theme), entity detail retrieval by CoLD ID, bulk table export, and specialist lookup by jurisdiction.

tags.name: Entities
tags.description: Paginated entity lists per type (court decisions, instruments, literature, etc.) with optional jurisdiction and theme filters. Useful for building filtered views or exporting entity catalogues.

tags.name: Statistics
tags.description: Aggregate metrics: record counts per entity type, jurisdiction-level answer coverage percentages, and per-table jurisdiction breakdowns.

tags.name: AI
tags.description: AI-powered helpers. Currently exposes a query classifier that maps free-text questions to predefined CoLD thematic categories.

tags.name: Case Analyzer
tags.description: Multi-step court decision analysis workflow. Upload a PDF, extract text, detect jurisdiction, and run AI-powered extraction (themes, citations, PIL provisions, etc.). Results stream as Server-Sent Events. Requires authentication.

tags.name: LandingPage
tags.description: Helper endpoints for the CoLD landing page, including jurisdiction data-availability flags.

tags.name: Sitemap
tags.description: Returns all indexable frontend URLs for search-engine sitemap generation.

tags.name: Suggestions
tags.description: Community data contributions. Authenticated users can submit new court decisions, instruments, or literature entries for moderator review. Editors/admins manage the moderation queue.

tags.name: Feedback
tags.description: Entity-level feedback. Users can report errors or suggest corrections on existing records. Editors/admins triage and resolve feedback items.
